note: Per invariant I4, this agent does not verify its own work. Handed off to /cdr:verify.
unmodified_but_relevant: ui/src/routes/GraphView.tsx (its own comment anticipated a future FilesView link; FilesAdminView catalog rows now link to /graph?path=<path>)

ui/src/api/mockClient.ts (left unmodified -- fetchFilesCatalog/fetchAdminStats stubs are now dead code, same precedent as fetchQueryResult/fetchGraphNeighbors after 6.1.2/6.1.3, confirmed via git show --stat on 71cb1bb and 7bb8af4)

naming_and_route_decision.question: 6.1.1's verifier flagged FilesView.tsx/AdminView.tsx as placeholders needing consolidation into issue #30's named FilesAdminView.tsx.
naming_and_route_decision.resolution: Consolidated the COMPONENT, not the routes: created a single ui/src/routes/FilesAdminView.tsx that fetches both /files and /admin and renders catalog + stats together. App.tsx's two existing top-level routes (/files, /admin) and nav links are both kept, both now rendering the same FilesAdminView instance. Rationale documented in architecture-discovery.md's 'Route/consolidation decision' section: issue #30 names a singular file and describes one component test mocking both endpoints; keeping both route paths avoids breaking anything (e.g. QueryView's citation links) that already targets /files.

path_deep_link_resolution.question: 6.1.2's verifier flagged QueryView.tsx's citation links to /files?path=<citation> as a dead end since FilesView.tsx never read the param.
path_deep_link_resolution.resolution: FilesAdminView.tsx reads ?path= via useSearchParams() (same convention as GraphView.tsx's 6.1.3 deep link). Unlike GraphView (which refetches by path), /files is not parameterized by path in the defined contract -- FilesAdminView fetches the full catalog once, then marks the matching row with data-highlighted="true" (see FilesAdminView.tsx's `highlightPath`/`isHighlighted` logic) and scrolls it into view via a guarded scrollIntoView call. Covered by FilesAdminView.test.tsx's 'highlights the catalog row matching a ?path= deep link' and 'does not highlight any row when no ?path= is present' tests.

wire_contract_disclosed_choice.confirmed_no_go_handler: api/routes/query.go is the only Go HTTP handler in the repo; api/main.go's newMux only registers a single /query route. No /files or /admin handler exists (same situation GraphView.tsx found for /graph in 6.1.3).
wire_contract_disclosed_choice.defined_contract: GET /files -> { files: [{ path, status: ACTIVE|SPLITTING|SPLIT|REDIRECT, sizeBytes, lastModified }] }; GET /admin -> { fileCount, statusCounts: { ACTIVE, SPLITTING, SPLIT, REDIRECT } }; 4xx/5xx plain-text error body, same convention as /query and /graph.
wire_contract_disclosed_choice.status_enum_source: docs/LLD/catalog.md CatalogRecord.status: ACTIVE | SPLITTING | SPLIT | REDIRECT.

acceptance_criteria_pointers.AC1_catalog_listing: ui/src/routes/FilesAdminView.tsx lines ~186-224 (catalog render); ui/src/routes/FilesAdminView.test.tsx 'renders catalog rows and corpus stats...' test
acceptance_criteria_pointers.AC2_corpus_stats: ui/src/routes/FilesAdminView.tsx lines ~161-179 (admin stats render); same test
acceptance_criteria_pointers.AC3_test_spec: ui/src/routes/FilesAdminView.test.tsx (mocks fetch for both /files and /admin in one beforeEach)

self_consistency.test: npm test -- --run -- pass, 4 test files / 20 tests total (App.test.tsx 6, FilesAdminView.test.tsx 6, QueryView.test.tsx 3, GraphView.test.tsx 5)
